# Council first-contact templates (F1.7)

> **Source:** EXEC-v1 §11 ("First-contact protocol"). The architect
> uses these templates to structure the first conversation with each
> council pillar candidate. Drafted by the build agent per the
> architect's authorisation in CLAUDE.md ("Drafting (not finalising)
> candidate conversations, letters, and agreements per EXEC §11,
> §15, §19 templates"). The architect adapts to the actual
> candidate; placeholders in `{{ ... }}` are filled at use time.

## Files

| File                                                     | Purpose                                                                 |
| -------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------- |
| [00-one-page-brief.md](00-one-page-brief.md)             | Bilingual one-page handout (FR recto / EN verso) — EXEC §11.5           |
| [10-pillar-governance.md](10-pillar-governance.md)       | First-contact opening dialogue, governance pillar, FR + EN — EXEC §11.2 |
| [11-pillar-judicial.md](11-pillar-judicial.md)           | First-contact opening dialogue, judicial pillar, FR + EN                |
| [12-pillar-civil-society.md](12-pillar-civil-society.md) | First-contact opening dialogue, civil-society pillar, FR + EN           |
| [13-pillar-audit.md](13-pillar-audit.md)                 | First-contact opening dialogue, audit pillar, FR + EN                   |
| [14-pillar-technical.md](14-pillar-technical.md)         | First-contact opening dialogue, technical pillar, FR + EN               |

## Hard rules at the top of each template (EXEC §11.3)

In every first conversation, the architect MUST NOT:

- Name the other 4 candidates or the shortlist.
- Promise immunity from prosecution or political reprisal.
- Over-quote SRD section numbers (mention once, in passing).
- Negotiate compensation (the role is unpaid by design).
- Commit to a launch deadline (the launch waits for the council).

The templates carry these prohibitions in their headers; the architect
re-reads them before each meeting.

## Workflow per candidate

1. Identify candidate; vet against EXEC §10 worksheet.
2. Schedule the first meeting per EXEC §11.1 (in person, neutral
   venue, 60–90 min).
3. Use the pillar-specific opening from `1*-pillar-*.md`. Listen for
   the three first concerns and their order/tone (EXEC §11.4
   diagnostic).
4. Within 48h, send the brief at `00-one-page-brief.md` by email.
5. Within 7–10 days, follow up exactly once if no response.
6. If yes: schedule second conversation specifically for the harder
   questions; the EXEC §12 commitment letter is signed at the END
   of THAT meeting, not the first.

## What this directory does NOT contain

- Specific candidate names. Filling a candidate's name in a template
  and committing it would (a) breach EXEC §11.3 ("do not name the
  other 4"), and (b) leak shortlist intent into the audit chain.
  The architect prints from a private working copy.
- Compensation terms. The role is unpaid by design.
- The actual EXEC §12 commitment letter. That is a separate
  template (TBD) for the second-conversation close.
- Decisions about who is approached. F1.1–F1.5 in
  [docs/work-program/PHASE-1-COMPLETION.md](../../work-program/PHASE-1-COMPLETION.md)
  remain architect-blocked; templates only support the conversation,
  not the candidate selection.
